text: Mobile app development has grown rapidly in recent years. One key concept is responsive design, which ensures an app works well on different screen sizes, from phones to tablets. Developers must choose between native and cross-platform approaches. Native apps are built for a specific system like iOS or Android, offering better performance and access to device features, but they require separate development for each platform. Cross-platform tools, such as React Native, allow a single codebase for multiple systems, saving time and money, though sometimes at the cost of performance. After development, apps are submitted to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store. Here, they must meet guidelines to be approved. A fast, stable app with good performance is crucial for user satisfaction.
